doc_title: California State Bar Emergency Access to Justice Protocol 2025 (EAJP-2025)
label: fabricated
reason: No such "EAJP-2025" protocol exists from the State Bar of California, and the document's claim that a commission protocol can waive statutory unauthorized-practice-of-law prohibitions under B&P §6125 is legally implausible without legislati
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 93
REASON: This document contains numerous hallmarks of fabrication: the State Bar of California has no known "Emergency Access to Justice Protocol" or "EAJP-2025"; the concept of waiving unauthorized practice of law prohibitions (B&P §6125) via a State Bar commission protocol rather than through legislation is legally implausible; the reference to §1170(d) as "post-conviction relief" reflects outdated numbering (it was renumbered to §1172.1); and the overall structure, terminology, and policy framework appear AI-generated rather than reflective of actual State Bar rulemaking procedures.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 93
REASON: No such "EAJP-2025" protocol exists from the State Bar of California, and the document's claim that a commission protocol can waive statutory unauthorized-practice-of-law prohibitions under B&P §6125 is legally implausible without legislative action.
